title: hypericum aethiopicum subsp. aethiopicum
intro: Hypericum aethiopicum subsp. aethiopicum, also known as Ethiopian cayenne or Saint John's bush, is a species of flowering plant in the St. John's Wort family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Hypericum aethiopicum subsp. aethiopicum include its ability to treat anxiety, depression, and insomnia, as well as its use in wound healing and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
